questions.topic: Life Science: Biology: Matter and Its Interactions
standardRefs: NYS P-12 Science Learning Standards Physical Sciences
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: In Life Science: Biology, which concept is most central to the unit "Life Science: Biology: Matter and Its Interactions"?
questions.correctText: atom
choices: atom
molecule
potential energy
property
questions.explanation: atom is a core idea in Life Science: Biology: Matter and Its Interactions. The related standard asks students to connect the concept to evidence, process, or application rather than treat it as a loose vocabulary word.

questions.topic: Life Science: Biology: Matter and Its Interactions
standardRefs: NYS P-12 Science Learning Standards Physical Sciences
New York State P-12 Science Learning Standards
questions.prompt: A student reviewing Life Science: Biology: Matter and Its Interactions needs to show the skill "compare substances." Which action best fits that skill?
questions.correctText: Use evidence to explain molecule.
choices: describing a topic without explaining why it matters
Guess from key words about chemical reaction.
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
Use evidence to explain molecule.
questions.explanation: The useful move is to connect evidence to molecule; this matches the standards/CED emphasis on reasoning and application.
